ingredients.explanation: Plain water — it's the base of almost every skincare product and helps dissolve other ingredients.
A form of Vitamin B3 that shrinks the look of pores, fades dark spots, and calms redness. It's a superstar ingredient for acne-prone skin.
concerns: Very high concentrations (above 10%) may cause flushing in sensitive skin

skin_type_notes.sensitive: Generally well-tolerated; start with lower concentrations

ingredients.explanation: An oil-soluble acid that gets deep inside pores to dissolve the gunk that causes blackheads and pimples. It's the go-to ingredient for acne.
concerns: Can dry out skin if overused

skin_type_notes.oily: Excellent — clears pores and reduces shine
skin_type_notes.dry: Use with caution — can be too drying; follow with moisturizer
skin_type_notes.combination: Great for oily zones; use targeted application on dry areas
skin_type_notes.sensitive: Start slowly; may be irritating at high concentrations

ingredients.explanation: A powerful acne-fighter that kills the bacteria responsible for causing pimples. Very effective but can be harsh.
concerns: Can be very drying and irritating

skin_type_notes.dry: High risk of irritation; use sparingly as spot treatment
skin_type_notes.combination: Good as spot treatment; avoid dry areas
skin_type_notes.sensitive: Often too harsh; try alternatives like niacinamide first

ingredients.explanation: A Vitamin A derivative that speeds up skin cell turnover, clears pores, and reduces acne scars over time. One of the most proven skincare ingredients.

skin_type_notes.dry: Start with low concentration and buffer with moisturizer
skin_type_notes.combination: Good; apply to oily zones first
skin_type_notes.sensitive: Start very slow (1-2 nights/week); use lowest available concentration

ingredients.explanation: A gentler form of Vitamin A. It converts to retinol in the skin, so it's less potent but also less irritating.
concerns: May not be strong enough for active acne

ingredients.explanation: A moisture magnet that pulls water into your skin and keeps it plump and hydrated. It holds up to 1000x its weight in water.
concerns: In very dry climates, can pull moisture from skin if no occlusive is used

skin_type_notes.dry: Essential — use under a richer moisturizer

ingredients.explanation: A simple, effective moisturizer that draws water to your skin. It's gentle, non-irritating, and found in almost every product.
An acid that removes dead skin cells from the surface, revealing brighter, smoother skin underneath. Great for fading acne marks.
concerns: Don't combine with other strong actives

skin_type_notes.dry: Use at low concentration; always moisturize after
skin_type_notes.sensitive: May be too harsh; try lactic acid instead

ingredients.explanation: A gentler acid than glycolic that exfoliates while also hydrating. Good for beginners or sensitive skin.

skin_type_notes.sensitive: One of the gentler acids available

ingredients.explanation: Pure Vitamin C that brightens skin, fades dark spots from acne, and protects against free radical damage.
concerns: Unstable — oxidizes in light and air
May irritate sensitive skin at high %

ingredients.explanation: A multi-tasker that kills acne bacteria, fades dark spots, and calms redness — all with very low irritation. Great for sensitive, acne-prone skin.
concerns: High concentrations require prescription in some countries

skin_type_notes.sensitive: One of the most tolerable acne-fighting ingredients

ingredients.explanation: A lipid that naturally exists in your skin. In products, ceramides repair the skin barrier, preventing moisture loss and protecting against irritants.
A silicone that creates a smooth, protective film on skin. It locks in moisture and gives products a silky feel.
concerns: Some people find it can trap sebum; debate around comedogenicity in acne-prone skin

skin_type_notes.oily: May not suit everyone; patch test recommended

ingredients.explanation: Added purely for smell — it has no skin benefit and is one of the most common causes of skin irritation and allergic reactions.

skin_type_notes.oily: May trigger breakouts in acne-prone skin

ingredients.explanation: A drying alcohol that can strip your skin's natural moisture barrier. It makes products feel lightweight but causes long-term dryness and irritation.

skin_type_notes.oily: May seem helpful short-term but causes rebound oil production

ingredients.explanation: A strong detergent that creates foam. It cleans effectively but strips the skin of its natural oils, causing dryness and irritation.

skin_type_notes.dry: Avoid — will make dryness much worse

ingredients.explanation: Similar to SLS but slightly gentler. Still a stripping cleanser — better versions use milder surfactants like sodium cocoyl isethionate.
A preservative that prevents bacteria and mold growth in products. Safe in the low concentrations used in cosmetics according to most regulatory bodies.
benefits: Keeps product safe from microbial growth
concerns: Weak estrogen mimic — ongoing research; currently considered safe at cosmetic concentrations
Some people prefer to avoid as a precaution

ingredients.explanation: A common, widely-used preservative that keeps products fresh. Generally considered safe and gentler than many alternatives.
concerns: Very high concentrations may irritate; cosmetic use is well within safe limits

ingredients.explanation: A physical sunscreen mineral that sits on top of the skin and reflects UV rays. Also has anti-inflammatory and mild anti-acne properties.
concerns: Can leave white cast on darker skin tones

skin_type_notes.sensitive: One of the best sunscreen options

ingredients.explanation: A physical mineral sunscreen that blocks UV rays. Gentle and non-irritating, often used in mineral sunscreens.
A plant extract known as 'cica' that calms irritated skin, helps heal acne scars, and reduces redness. Very popular in K-beauty.
A natural oil with antibacterial properties that can help fight acne bacteria. Effective but must be diluted — never apply undiluted.
concerns: May cause contact dermatitis in some

skin_type_notes.sensitive: Patch test first; may be too irritating

ingredients.explanation: A thick barrier ingredient that seals in moisture and protects the skin. Despite its greasy feel, it's non-comedogenic and helps healing.
concerns: Not suitable as a daily moisturizer for oily skin

skin_type_notes.oily: May feel too heavy; better as a targeted occlusive
skin_type_notes.dry: Excellent for very dry or damaged skin

ingredients.explanation: A rich, fatty butter that deeply moisturizes dry skin. Can be heavy for acne-prone skin, especially if oily.
concerns: Not ideal for oily or acne-prone skin

ingredients.explanation: Technically a wax, not an oil. It mimics the skin's natural sebum and is one of the most skin-compatible oils for acne-prone skin.
A very popular natural oil that's highly moisturizing but has a high comedogenic rating, meaning it's very likely to clog pores.
concerns: Not recommended for acne-prone skin on the face

ingredients.explanation: A synthetic emollient that gives products a silky, non-greasy feel. However, it has a high comedogenic rating and frequently triggers breakouts.
Vitamin E — an antioxidant that protects skin cells from damage and helps moisturize. Often used to enhance other antioxidants like Vitamin C.
concerns: Very high concentrations may clog pores in some people

ingredients.explanation: Pro-Vitamin B5 that attracts moisture, soothes irritated skin, and speeds up healing. Great for post-acne recovery.
A calming ingredient that soothes irritated skin, promotes cell renewal, and speeds up healing. Very gentle and well-tolerated.
Aloe vera soothes sunburn, calms irritation, and lightly hydrates. Gentle and non-comedogenic.
concerns: Some people are allergic; patch test recommended

ingredients.explanation: A zinc compound that reduces excess oil production and has mild antibacterial effects. Particularly helpful for oily, acne-prone skin.

skin_type_notes.oily: Excellent for reducing oil and shine

ingredients.explanation: A common moisturizing ingredient that also helps other ingredients absorb into the skin. Generally safe but can irritate very sensitive skin.
concerns: Can irritate sensitive skin at high concentrations

skin_type_notes.sensitive: Patch test if you have very reactive skin

ingredients.explanation: A polymer used to thicken products and give gels their gel-like texture. It's inert and has no direct skin effect.
A natural component of skin's own moisturizing factor. It draws water into the skin, improving hydration.
Used to give products a smooth, film-like finish. Mostly a texture agent.
concerns: May contain trace acrylamide — a concern raised in some studies but generally considered safe in cosmetics

ingredients.explanation: A plant extract that brightens skin and fades dark spots, including post-acne hyperpigmentation. Also calms redness.
Rich in antioxidants (EGCG) that protect skin from environmental damage and reduce redness. Great supporting ingredient.
